sections.paragraph:
κύπτω 1 aor. ἔκυψα bend (oneself) down (so Hom. et al.; LXX; En 14:25; JosAs 18:7) Mk 1:7. κάτω κ. bend down to the ground (Aristoph., Vesp. 279; Theophr., Char. 24, 8; Chariton 2, 3, 6; 2, 5, 5; Pel.-Leg. 23, 18) J 8:6, 8 v.l.; AcPl Ha 4, 12.—M-M. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Chariton Chariton , scriptor eroticus (writer of romances) I–II a.d.—List 5

Pel.-Leg. Pel.-Leg. = Legenden der heiligen Pelagia, V? a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

AcPl AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 575
